lede: A look at July 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including John Quincy Adams and Heini Salonen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Spain national football team
events.desc: In Johannesburg, Spain defeat the Netherlands 1–0 after extra time to win their first FIFA World Cup title.

events.title: Islamism
events.desc: The Islamist militia group Al-Shabaab carries out multiple suicide bombings in Kampala, Uganda, killing 74 people and injuring 85 others.

events.title: Italy national football team
events.desc: Italy defeats West Germany 3–1 to win the FIFA World Cup.

events.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
events.desc: To Kill a Mockingbird by Harper Lee is first published, in the United States.

events.title: Robert F. Kennedy Bridge
events.desc: The Triborough Bridge in New York City is opened to traffic.

events.title: USS Nevada (BB-36)
events.desc: The US Navy launches the USS Nevada (BB-36) as its first standard-type battleship.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Fort Stevens; Confederate forces attempt to invade Washington, D.C.

events.title: London Waterloo station
events.desc: Waterloo railway station in London opens.

events.title: Jean-Louis Pons
events.desc: French astronomer Jean-Louis Pons makes his first comet discovery. In the next 27 years he discovers another 36 comets, more than any other person in history.

events.title: Michael I Rangabe
events.desc: Byzantine emperor Michael I, under threat by conspiracies, abdicates in favor of his general Leo the Armenian, and becomes a monk (under the name Athanasius).

births.name: John Quincy Adams
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 6th President of the United States

births.name: Gough Whitlam
births.line: Australian lieutenant, lawyer, and politician, 21st Prime Minister of Australia

births.name: Julia King, Baroness Brown of Cambridge

deaths.text: Laurence Olivier, English actor, director, and producer
Joachim I Nestor, Elector of Brandenburg, Elector of Brandenburg from 1499 to 1535
Otto I, Duke of Bavaria, Duke of Bavaria

observances.text: Day of the Flemish Community (Flemish Community of Belgium)
